title: Corner Wooden Mantel
summary: A quick and easy DIY tutorial for a wooden mantle piece to upgrade your corner mantle.
description: A quick and easy DIY tutorial for a wooden mantle piece to upgrade your corner mantle.

steps.title: Measure Mantel
steps.body: Measure the face where your mantel will be installed against or note the length of your desired mantel “X” in attached plans (under "Extras" tab).

steps.title: Measure Corner Angles
steps.body: This mantel is designed to be installed in a corner, measure the angle of each corner where the mantel with butt up against the wall these will be your “A” and “B” angles.

steps.title: Cut Front Board
steps.body: Cut your “FRONT” board to “X” length with the mitre saw and 6" width with your table saw. Cut your “A” and “B” angles on the ends of the board (see drawings/photos for clarification).

steps.title: Cut Top and Bottom Boards
steps.body:
Cut your “TOP” board. If you are tying into an existing mantel, dimension “Y” will take into account your existing overhang. Our mantel had a 4” overhang already.
For example: 6” (length of “BOTTOM” board) minus 4” (length of existing overhang) equals 2”, which was our “Y” dimension.
If you don’t have any overhang to tie into, your “Y” dimension on your “TOP” board will be the same width as the “BOTTOM” board (which is 6”).

steps.title: Drill Pocket Holes
steps.body: Using your Kreg Jig® R3 drill your pocket holes as noted on the drawings. Set your depth on the Jig to 3/4". *Note holes can be added or removed depending on the length of your mantel*

steps.title: Cut Mounting Blocks
steps.body:
Cut three 2x8" blocks that you will use to mount your mantel. See plans for placement.
*Note mounting blocks can be added or removed depending on the length of your mantel*

steps.title: Attach Mounting Blocks
steps.body: Use Kreg Jig to drill holes to allow blocks to mount to the wall.

steps.title: Drill Holes
steps.body:
Drill 3 holes into the "BOTTOM" board (to align with your mounting blocks) with a 1/8” diameter drill bit.
Counter sink 1/4" deep with a 3/8” diameter Forstner bit. These holes will allow you to attach the mantel to the 2x4" blocks mounted on the wall.

steps.title: Assemble Mantel
steps.body: Assemble "TOP", "BOTTOM", and "FRONT" boards using the previously drilled pockets (step 5). Use wood glue on all adjacent edges.

steps.title: Finishing
steps.body: Putty and sand mantle as needed. Stain using the colour of your choice and apply varnish once stain is dry.

steps.title: Install Mantel
steps.body: Install your mantel onto your mounting blocks. Kreg pocket joints can be used on the top surface and plugged with Kreg plugs (type as required to match your existing surface).
